raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Californians can expect significant advancements in gender and racial equity under AB 2419.",
    "AB 2419 directs 40% of federal Infrastructure funding to disadvantaged and low-income communities.",
    "The Gender Equity Policy Institute found that 6 in 10 Californians live in communities prioritized for infrastructure investment under AB 2419.",
    "In rural Central Valley and northern California Counties, more than 7 in 10 residents live in communities targeted for investment under AB 2419.",
    "Nearly 60% of all Californians live in low-income and disadvantaged communities that are disproportionately Black, Brown, and Indigenous.",
    "The California Justice40 Act aims to create economic opportunities and uplift historically silenced voices in decision-making processes.",
    "Nearly 75% of residents in low-income and disadvantaged communities are Black, Latino, Asian American, or Native American.",
    "Women of color are overrepresented in low-income and disadvantaged communities, with 72% of all Black female and 77% of all Latina Californians living in these areas.",
    "The Justice40 Committee will include environmental justice, labor, and community representatives to guide California's infrastructure investments.",
    "California will receive at least $45 billion in infrastructure funds through the federal Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act."
  ]
}
```
